reviews.review_msg: The class is great if you want a real introduction to physical geography. I thought I was prepared for the lab exam, but it was harder than it seemed, dont skip studying for it. The prof is really passionate about the topics in the course so the lectures aren't too bad to sit through. Grading was fair and evenly weighted

Tara is a great professor and GEOG 111 is a smart choice for non-science students looking for a relatively easy B-SCI course. As long as you review lecture slides before tests and complete all the labs you will do well! Tara makes boring geography stuff as interesting as possible and is very reasonable in terms of quizzes/scheduling.

Tara's class organization was seamless, making the course navigation easy, especially during the strike this semester. Her lectures were information-heavy, but her slides were great. Sometimes quizzes focused only on 1 out of the many learning objectives. However, an amazing prof, definitely take class with her again! She's super cool and caring.

I found this class to be so so difficult. The TA I had did a very bad job at running my lab and explaining lab concepts. The labs and lectures are quite unrelated. The case study quizzes are so difficult and not entirely based on the learning objectives we are told to use as a study guide. Lab exam was so hard, I believe I failed. Not very happy.

Highly recommend this prof!!! Makes 2 hour lectures feel shorter because they are engaging and interesting, very clear syllabus and slides, labs relate to course content, and bonus no midterm or final for lecture content. Chiefs kiss

The class is difficult if you know nothing about geog. Must attend the classes to fill in the blank in lectures notes. Lecture notes are enough for theory questions, but there are something like drawing maps in final, which is unexpected because it's different from midterm. Thus, skimming the textbook is a good idea to prepare to the final

Faran is good at explaining but the material itself was boring for me. Tests were fair, may need to read the textbook though if you don't catch up everything. Other than that, everything you need for B grade range is lecture slides. Make sure that you know how  to complete assignments they will be in the final paper.

Content is interesting, but he makes the lectures dry and rambles on forever. Some of his exam questions are pretty random, based on things he barely touched on in class, but if you memorize all the lecture notes and diagrams you should be good

Lectures were very interesting. His tests were different in that we were able to choose which questions to write on. For example: choose 5 out of 8 words to write definitions for. The only negative feedback was he was very slow in marking and putting up lecture notes, assignments, grades. Other than that, great class!
Horrible. His jokes are crud and he is completelynarcisstic. Thinks he is being helpful but he is confusing and his notes are almost illagible.

if you need a WQB sci credit, this is not the class for you. Andrew is nice but the course is so heavy and the amount of lab work is simply not worth 3 credits, (should be 4 tbh) so just trust me cuz i was under the impression it was going to be a side class...I was wrong.

Very passionate professor. Lecture attendance is not mandatory but laboratory are and they're fun especially with the right set of people. Lecture and lab quizzes are easy just make sure to refresh weekly. Online quizzes or assignment on canvas can be a bit tricky due to the formatting so stay on top of that too. Overall, great prof!

NOT a gpa booster, imo too much work for 3 credits, should be 4 with a 2 hour lecture and 2 hour lab, I won't lie its so easy to get lost so make use of your TA's office hours if you have any questions, Bella the TA is goated

Andrew was amazing, and the course was very well-structured and easy to follow. He was approachable and understanding whenever students were confused or needed extra support. My only remark is that there are bi-weekly labs take up a lot of time but are only worth 2% each.
Very entertaining lectures. Pretty clear about what was gonna be on quizzes and tests

Gave short summaries of textbooks chapters instead of entire chapters. Clearly passionate. Mainly graded by 3 tests, labs, short weekly worksheets, and a lab final test. As long as you stay on top of things and do the short readings you'll be fine. Great prof held back by a terrible TA, the lab sections sucked. Test questions were often tricky.

I have not yet completed this class, but this is your sign to not take it unless you are a geography major. I have NEVER had a more unclear grading expierence, WAY too many labs quizzes, quizzes, finals and assignments and the canvas grade calculator does not work. VERY unorganized If you want a cool science credit for GPA, this is not the course.

Great guy, great lectures, super easy to follow. Labs were heavy and sometimes really tricky, but the TAs heelped a lot. Weekly lab quizzes online, but not too challenging. Overall, a very fun and interesting course

If you like GEOG, it's such a fun class. The exams were fairly easy as well. Did his best to explain the content and keep us engaged. The only confusing choice was giving us new material in the first half of the lecture before both exams, but otherwise I loved it.
I was annoyed at how this guy took so many geeky jabs at pre-Lyellian scientists who thought the earth was only a few thousand years old. Ian, you're an enlightened man. Get over it!

this is for geog213. Jonny is a great professor, it is clear to see that he understands the material at a deep level and that inspires you to do well in his course. Jonny is by far the best professor I had at SFU so far, if you have the option of taking one of his classes, do it.

I think the grading criteria could be clearer because I didnt understand a lot of the times why I lost marks on things (went to office hours too) but overall pretty standard for a 100 level course. Ive had worse and better before.

This was a simple class that Jonathan over complicated for no reason. I'm not sure if it was a lack of preparation or just not understanding how to teach. If I didn't have a geography background I would have done bad but luckily I learned a lot of this before
extremely boring lecturer

Seems passionate about Geography, but not a good professor at all. Extremely disorganized both on Canvas and lecturing, as well as pretty disrespectful to students, which made it difficult to want to answer questions in class or approach him to get help. I wish I took this with a different professor. Though, the lab portion with my TA was great! :)

Fantastic professor. Open book tests meant they were straightforward and low stress. Labs were easy as the TA gave the answer to each question after the whole class did it. Lab test based off the labs 1:1. Tests were made up of labelling diagrams (where diagrams were from the slides) and short written questions, which were easy as it was open book.
